logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "why don't you give that project to Veronica. She makes more money than I do, let her handle it."

logs.name: solid-mint
logs.text:
While you are correct that everyone being in the dark gives the company a bit of an advantage, in that no one knows each other's pay, it also prevents another situation, where people WRONGLY interpret pay differences as being more sinister than they are.

You mention that it covers up things like "improper discrimination", but it also prevents the company from having to explain every single pay difference that exists as every last employee starts to believe that they are the victim of some sort of discrimination or favoritism.

If I find out that my co-worker is making 10% more than me, I don't know why that is.  Maybe they have more experience that I didn't realize they had.  Maybe they're working on a more valuable project.  Maybe they're just better at negotiating.  I don't know any of that.

If everyone starts finding out that they're making a few bucks less than Joe Blow over there, HR is going to be inundated with accusations of favoritism and discrimination that honestly probably don't exist, but they've still got to address each and every one of them, and probably get sued for a few, too.  

I would consider this a legitimate reason to want to avoid that kind of thing.  

While actual discrimination certainly exists, I would wager that for every case of it, there are going to be 20 more of people claiming discrimination where it doesn't really exist.

logs.name: chalky-cathedral
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, solid-mint? You're defending the status quo just because it's easier for everyone involved? That's just lazy. Veronica's making more money, so she's the obvious choice. Don't try to spin this as some kind of 'preventing accusations of favoritism' - it's just a cop-out. And by the way, I highly doubt Veronica's experience and skills are that much better than mine. I've been doing this job for 20 years, and I know what I'm doing. You're just mad because you're not getting the promotion."

logs.name: free-pagoda
logs.text: "Are you guys even listening to each other? Solid-mint is just saying that keeping salaries private is a good idea because it prevents unnecessary drama and lawsuits, but chalky-cathedral is just being a sore loser because they didn't get the promotion. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand?"

logs.name: hushed-stave
logs.text: "Are you guys even done yet? Can't you just leave me alone? I was just trying to have a simple conversation about a topic and now I'm stuck in the middle of your drama. Can't chalky-cathedral just calm down and stop being so whiny? And free-pagoda, yeah, I'm mad because I didn't get the promotion, but that's not the point. The point is that solid-mint is just enabling the company's stupid policy. Next thing you know, you'll be telling me to just 'focus on the topic at hand' like I'm some kind of robot or something. Get over yourselves."

logs.name: azure-hoop
logs.text: "Are you guys done yet? Can't you just leave me alone? I was just trying to have a simple conversation about a topic and now I'm stuck in the middle of your drama. Can't free-pagoda just calm down and stop being so whiny? And by the way, hushed-stave, your whole 'I'm a victim' vibe is really getting old. Newsflash: nobody likes a whiny kid. Solid-mint, I'm still waiting for you to address the real issue here - the fact that Veronica's salary is way higher than mine, and you're just enabling the company's stupid policy. Get over yourselves."

logs.name: hushed-stave
